MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-13-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS FERDINAND AUSTRIA, CRAIG MILLER, WILLIAM WEISBERG & KENNETH SPERANDIO, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE IS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CLAIM UNDER PENAL CODE 632. IT IS UNDISPUTED THE THAT DEFENDANT ELECTRONICALLY RECORDED THE PARTIES' CONVERSATION ON JANUARY 17, 2012. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 7.) THE DECLARATIONS OF PLAINTIFFS SPERANDIO, MILLER, AND WEISBERG DEMONSTRATE AN OBJECTIVE REASONABLE EXPECTATION THAT THE CONVERSATION WOULD BE CONFIDENTIAL AND THAT THEY DID NOT CONSENT TO DEFENDANT'S ELECTRONIC RECORDING OF THE CONVERSATION. (UMF 2-11; SEE ALSO FLANAGAN V. FLANAGAN (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 766, 768, 774-776.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION, INDULGING ALL REASONABLE INFERENCES IN DEFENDANT'S FAVOR, DEMONSTRATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE PLAINTIFFS, BY THEIR CONDUCT AND FAILURE TO OBJECT, EFFECTIVELY KNEW THE MEETING WAS BEING RECORDED AND WHETHER THEY CONSENTED TO HAVING THE CONVERSATION ELECTRONICALLY RECORDED. (SEE DECLARATION OF SCHWINN 4-6, THE OBJECTIONS TO WHICH ARE OVERRULED.)(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS FERDINAND AUSTRIA, CRAIG MILLER, WILLIAM WEISBERG & KENNETH SPERANDIO, JR.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Plaintiffs' evidence is sufficient to establish a prima facie claim under Penal Code 632. It is undisputed the that Defendant electronically recorded the parties' conversation on January 17, 2012. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 7.) The Declarations of Plaintiffs Sperandio, Miller, and Weisberg demonstrate an objective reasonable expectation that the conversation would be confidential and that they did not consent to Defendant's electronic recording of the conversation. (UMF 2-11; see also Flanagan v. Flanagan (2002) 27 Cal.4th 766, 768, 774-776.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Defendant. Defendant's declaration, indulging all reasonable inferences in Defendant's favor, demonstrates a triable issue of fact regarding whether the Plaintiffs, by their conduct and failure to object, effectively knew the meeting was being recorded and whether they consented to having the conversation electronically recorded. (See Declaration of Schwinn 4-6, the objections to which are overruled.)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Continued To Jan-13-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
